title: [lora_l2.c:127] generate_eui64 relies on hwinfo_get_device_id positive for length
description: Function: generate_eui64 (static helper). Contract violation: The function checks 'hwid_len <= 0' at line 135 but hwinfo_get_device_id() returns ssize_t. If the function returned exactly 0 (empty device ID), the code would reject it (-ENODEV), which is correct. However, if it returned a very large positive value that exceeds sizeof(hwid), the check at line 141 'hwid_len > sizeof(hwid)' catches this. The cast to size_t at line 149 is safe because the check at 141 ensures hwid_len fits. This is correct code. Priority 3 - verified correct, no issue.
close_reason: Duplicate: Already fixed by tvfm.3. The generate_eui64 function at lora_l2.c:208-266 correctly handles all hwinfo_get_device_id return cases: negative returns (error codes) at line 216, zero returns at line 222, and values exceeding buffer size at line 227. Only valid positive lengths 1-32 proceed to the hash function. Build verified.
